2015 Year in Music

January 19, 2016

As I mentioned last year, I was leaning toward moving back to Beats Music. That came true in 2015 with the launch of Apple Music, the replacement for Beats Music. The service got off to a rough start, but incremental improvements have been released through out 2015 and I now have very few complaints.

Here are my favorite releases for 2015:

1. Kendrick Lamar - To Pimp a Butterfly (One of the best hip hop albums in the last 5 or 10 years, if not ever. Hi Chris!) 2. Tame Impala - Currents (Their strongest, most cohesive album yet.) 3. Ryan Adams - 1989 (Adams takes Taylor Swift’s songs in a new, melancholy direction and it’s amazing.) 4. Sleater-Kinney - No Cities to Love (One of the best live bands I’ve ever seen, returns from an almost 10 year break with one of their best.) 5. Foals - What Went Down (What Went Down combines the best of the dance-y post-punk with straight forward alt-rock to produce a great album.) 6. Discourse - Sanity Decays (One of the better live hardcore bands I’ve seen in a while and a pretty darn good album.) 7. Courtney Barnett - Sometimes I Sit and Think, Sometimes I Just Sit (Kind of like an Australian Liz Phair. So good.) 8. Chvrches - Every Open Eye (I am a sucker for this kind of indie-influenced pop music. Chvrches delivers every time.) 9. Beauty Pill - Beauty Pill Describes Things As They Are (Beauty Pill returns from a long hiatus to deliver an amazing, eclectic take on 2015.) 10. Saintseneca - Such Things (Not as mind blowing as Dark Arc, but that is asking a lot.)

What are your top 5 albums from 2015?

Bands I Saw in 2015

Last year was pretty amazing, music-wise.

  • Sleater-Kinney w/ Waxahatchee (Babeville)
  • Saintseneca (Babeville)
  • Desaparecidos w/ So So Glos (Rochester)
  • Grade, Chokehold, Ricochet, FU Pay Me, Pissing Match (Hamilton)
  • Blonde Redhead (Waiting Room)
  • Knapsack (Toronto)
  • Kool A.D. (Philly)
  • Waxahatchee w/ Girlpool (Toronto)
  • Neutral Milk Hotel (Babeville)
  • St. Vincent (Rochester)
  • Harm's Way, Discourse, Longest War (Toronto)

On tap, so far, for 2016:

  • Neko Case
  • Judge, World Be Free, Longest War, Wreckage, Black X
  • Snapcase, Endpoint, Grade

What was your favorite concert in 2015?
